items.description: A single user request tracks bounded PTC recovery state and can never exceed one automatic recovery attempt.
steps: Trigger a qualifying async-wrapper failure on the first code_execution attempt.
Verify request-scoped state records the first failure and allows at most one automatic recovery attempt.
Start a new user request and verify recovery state is reset to a clean baseline.

items.description: Async-only code_execution failures are classified deterministically and produce a minimal corrective recovery prompt.
steps: Feed representative failure inputs for missing-await and async-wrapper-iterated errors into the classifier.
Verify unrelated SyntaxError and NameError inputs classify to null.
Verify each supported failure class maps to one stable, minimal recovery prompt.

items.description: The request lifecycle integrates async-only auto-recovery without rewriting code, changing user intent, or broadening file-selection semantics.
steps: Run a prompt that triggers an eligible async-wrapper failure and verify the next turn receives only the targeted corrective append.
Verify no second automatic recovery append occurs on a second failure in the same request.
Verify literal zero-match path cases do not trigger auto-recovery in the initial implementation.

items.description: Ephemeral request telemetry and code_execution result metadata expose routing, recovery, and terminal-state details without introducing a persistent sink.
steps: Execute a recovered request and inspect tool result details for routing and recovery metadata.
Execute a non-recovered request and verify telemetry still records first-path, attempt count, and terminal state.
Verify no new persistent telemetry artifact is written outside benchmark result files.

items.description: A JSON eval corpus under .pi/evals/ptc can define routing and recovery expectations deterministically.
steps: Create JSON eval case files for positive PTC routing, negative direct routing, mutation negative controls, and async recovery scenarios.
Validate each case against the documented schema and required fields.
Verify malformed case files fail validation deterministically.

items.description: A benchmark runner emits deterministic JSON result records for each eval case and provider-model run.
steps: Run the benchmark runner against a seeded eval subset for at least one configured model.
Verify each result record includes case_id, observed_first_path, success, recovery_attempted, failure_class, total_tokens, and duration_ms.
Verify baseline comparison reports routing or recovery regressions without mutating source planning docs.

items.description: Regression tests block unsafe routing and recovery behavior changes.
steps: Run unit tests for routing classification, recovery classification, prompt generation, and max-attempt clamping.
Run integration tests proving one recovery attempt maximum, mutation prompt exclusion, and per-request state reset.
Verify the full repository test suite passes after the new coverage is added.

items.description: User-facing documentation explains the bounded async-only recovery model and JSON eval workflow accurately.
steps: Update the relevant documentation files after implementation behavior is stable.
Verify the docs describe async-only recovery scope, ephemeral telemetry, and JSON eval artifacts correctly.
Verify documentation changes do not claim native Anthropic protocol parity or unsupported recovery behaviors.
